quoteFirst: It is said that it was a blind piper; but, as John told me,       the fellow was not blind, but an ignorant, weak, poor man, and       usually walked his rounds
quoteLast: and went       piping along from door to door, and the people usually took him       in at public-houses where they knew him, and would give him drink       and victuals, and sometimes farthings; and he in return would       pipe and sing and talk simply, which diverted the people; and       thus he lived.
